title: cistus heterophyllus subsp. heterophyllus
intro: Cistus heterophyllus subsp. heterophyllus, also known as the Cretan or Greek Rockrose, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Cistaceae. It is a small shrub with grayish-green leaves and pink or white flowers that have been used in traditional medicine for their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The plant is used to treat various health issues, including respiratory problems, wounds, and skin conditions, due to its high content of flavonoids and saponins. Cistus heterophyllus subsp. heterophyllus is often prepared in herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures to utilize its medicinal properties and provide health benefits such as reduced inflammation and improved immune function.
